jobText:
Front-End Developer (next.js)
 віддалено
About the Company & Product

We are a rapidly growing product company entering the global gaming entertainment market. Our mission is to bring innovative business solutions to the iGaming industry by building cutting-edge technology products. Our core product is a SaaS gaming platform with 200+ microservices that powers a seamless gaming experience for millions of users worldwide. The platform provides a full suite of iGaming solutions, including game management, payments, analytics, CRM, and more.

Meet Your Future Team

We have an open management culture where leaders share a clear product vision rather than simply assigning tasks. We believe people do their best work when they understand where the team is going and why their contribution matters. Initiative is always welcome, and everyone is encouraged to share ideas, suggest improvements, and influence how things are built.

Ready for a career upgrade?

We are looking for a Front-End Developer to provide high-quality support to stakeholders and offer timely advice on the functionality.

What you’ll be doing

Develop new features for web applications using React and Next.js
Build responsive, cross-browser UI using TailwindCSS
Work with server-side rendering (SSR) and server components, optimize Web Vitals
Integrate with backend via GraphQL API (Apollo Client), optimize queries and caching
Contribute to CI/CD setup and build processes
Identify and implement improvements to code quality and application performance
Participate in sprint planning, collaborate with QA, Backend, PM, and Design teams, and take part in code reviews
You have these superpowers

3+ years of experience in web development with React
Strong knowledge of JavaScript (ES6+) and TypeScript
Experience with Next.js (SSR, App Router, Server Components)
Experience working with GraphQL API and Apollo Client
Strong knowledge of HTML5 and CSS, experience with TailwindCSS
Ability to build performant, responsive, and cross-browser web applications
Understanding of how browsers and web servers work; experience with debugging tools (DevTools, Network, Performance)
Experience writing unit tests (Jest or similar)
Experience with Git
Ability to write clean, maintainable code
English sufficient for reading documentation and technical communication
Strong communication skills and ability to work in a team
Nice to have

Experience with performance optimization (bundle analysis, profiling, Web Vitals)
Experience with REST API and WebSocket
Experience with Sentry or similar monitoring tools
Understanding of feature flags and trunk-based development
Experience working with various Web APIs
Experience using AI tools effectively in web development
Proactive mindset and willingness to grow professionally
Readiness to participate in architecture discussions and suggest improvements

must_have: 3+ years of experience in web development with React
Strong knowledge of JavaScript (ES6+) and TypeScript
Experience with Next.js (SSR, App Router, Server Components)
Experience working with GraphQL API and Apollo Client
Strong knowledge of HTML5 and CSS, experience with TailwindCSS
Ability to build performant, responsive, and cross-browser web applications
Understanding of how browsers and web servers work; experience with debugging tools (DevTools, Network, Performance)
Experience writing unit tests (Jest or similar)
Ability to write clean, maintainable code
English sufficient for reading documentation and technical communication
nice_to_have: Experience with performance optimization (bundle analysis, profiling, Web Vitals)
Experience with REST API and WebSocket
Experience with Sentry or similar monitoring tools
Understanding of feature flags and trunk-based development
Experience working with various Web APIs
Experience using AI tools effectively in web development
Readiness to participate in architecture discussions and suggest improvements
responsibilities: Develop new features for web applications using React and Next.js
Build responsive, cross-browser UI using TailwindCSS
Work with server-side rendering (SSR) and server components, optimize Web Vitals
Integrate with backend via GraphQL API (Apollo Client), optimize queries and caching
Contribute to CI/CD setup and build processes
Identify and implement improvements to code quality and application performance
Participate in sprint planning, collaborate with QA, Backend, PM, and Design teams, and take part in code reviews
soft_signals: Leaders share product vision rather than assigning tasks
Everyone encouraged to share ideas and suggest improvements
Proactive mindset and willingness to grow professionally
red_flags: iGaming/gambling industry — may be a concern for some candidates
Broad scope: expected to contribute to CI/CD, architecture discussions, and cross-team collaboration alongside core front-end work
Seniority title is 'Developer' but 3+ years and architecture involvement suggest strong-middle expectations

tailored.headline: Front-End Developer | React, Next.js, TypeScript, GraphQL, TailwindCSS
tailored.summary: Front-End Developer with 5+ years of experience building performant, responsive web applications with React and Next.js. Comfortable delivering complex features end-to-end — from API integration with GraphQL and Apollo Client to SSR optimization, Web Vitals tuning, and unit testing with Jest. Experienced collaborating closely with QA, Backend, PM, and Design teams in fast-moving product environments.

bullets: Designed and implemented a monorepo architecture using Nx to unify three products — marketplace, admin dashboard, and landing pages — maximizing UI component and business logic reuse
Developed core features of a crypto marketplace (50K+ MAU), including secure authentication, a multi-step order creation workflow, and an advanced search with dynamic filtering
Built marketing landing pages with Next.js using SSR and semantic HTML, integrating analytics, tracking scripts, and A/B testing configurations that boosted user conversion by ~15%
Implemented a real-time P2P communication layer using WebSockets, enabling live chat messaging and transaction-status synchronization between buyers and sellers
Delivered a management and arbitration admin panel with financial dashboards, role-based access control (RBAC), and subscription management tools
Contributed to CI/CD pipeline configuration, production monitoring setup, and code review workflows to maintain delivery quality
Built an SEO-optimized marketing landing page from scratch using Next.js with SSR, optimizing page speed and search rankings to support B2B user acquisition
Integrated GraphQL API via Apollo Client across the product, optimizing queries and caching strategies to reduce redundant network requests
Refactored the data-fetching layer using TanStack Query, adding caching, background revalidation, and consistent loading-state handling across the application
Improved client-side performance (LCP, TTI) through code-splitting and lazy loading, reducing bundle size by ~10% and widget load times by ~15%
Developed and maintained an embeddable chat widget using Shadow DOM for clean DOM containment and WebSockets for real-time AI response delivery
Integrated Sentry for error tracking and production monitoring, enabling faster debugging and improved application observability
Built responsive, cross-browser UI components using TailwindCSS and Material UI, ensuring consistent rendering across devices and browsers
Built an SEO-optimized marketing landing page using Next.js with semantic HTML5, custom meta tags, and responsive layouts to maximize search ranking and conversion
Developed data-heavy analytical dashboards with responsive tables (1000+ rows), pagination, transaction filtering, and interactive financial charts for real-time revenue tracking
Implemented multi-step configuration forms with advanced validation for donation thresholds, media file constraints, and profanity filtering
Integrated REST APIs and WebSockets for global state management using Redux, supporting reliable real-time alert distribution
Delivered pixel-perfect, cross-browser layouts from Figma designs using SCSS, ensuring consistent performance across all device sizes

coverage.requirement: 3+ years of experience in web development with React
coverage.evidence: 5+ years total; React used across all 3 employers (KYC Market, AMO FINTECH, DonateSystem)

coverage.requirement: Strong knowledge of JavaScript (ES6+) and TypeScript
coverage.evidence: TypeScript listed as primary language across all roles; used in every employer's tech stack

coverage.requirement: Experience with Next.js (SSR, App Router, Server Components)
coverage.evidence: Next.js used at KYC Market and AMO FINTECH for SSR landing pages and marketplace features; SSR explicitly mentioned

coverage.requirement: Experience working with GraphQL API and Apollo Client
coverage.evidence: Apollo Client and GraphQL explicitly listed in AMO FINTECH tech stack and skills

coverage.requirement: Strong knowledge of HTML5 and CSS, experience with TailwindCSS
coverage.evidence: HTML, CSS, SCSS, TailwindCSS all listed in skills; TailwindCSS used at AMO FINTECH

coverage.requirement: Ability to build performant, responsive, and cross-browser web applications
coverage.evidence: Performance optimization (LCP, TTI, bundle size -10%, widget load -15%) at AMO FINTECH; cross-browser layouts at DonateSystem

coverage.requirement: Understanding of how browsers and web servers work; experience with debugging tools (DevTools, Network, Performance)
coverage.evidence: Implied by performance profiling (LCP, TTI, bundle analysis) and Sentry integration; DevTools not explicitly named

coverage.requirement: Experience writing unit tests (Jest or similar)
coverage.evidence: Jest, Vitest, React Testing Library, and Playwright all listed in skills and KYC Market / AMO FINTECH tech stacks
Code review workflows mentioned at KYC Market; Git is a standard implied tool across all roles

coverage.requirement: Ability to write clean, maintainable code
coverage.evidence: Code audit and bug fixing at AMO FINTECH; code review workflows at KYC Market; refactoring initiatives across roles

coverage.requirement: English sufficient for reading documentation and technical communication
coverage.evidence: English listed as Professional level in spoken languages

coverage.requirement: Experience with performance optimization (bundle analysis, profiling, Web Vitals)
coverage.evidence: LCP, TTI optimization; bundle size reduced by ~10%; widget load times reduced by ~15% at AMO FINTECH

coverage.requirement: Experience with REST API and WebSocket
coverage.evidence: WebSockets used at KYC Market and AMO FINTECH; REST API integration at DonateSystem

coverage.requirement: Experience with Sentry or similar monitoring tools
coverage.evidence: Sentry integrated at AMO FINTECH for error tracking and production monitoring

coverage.requirement: Understanding of feature flags and trunk-based development
Experience working with various Web APIs
coverage.evidence: Shadow DOM used for chat widget at AMO FINTECH; WebSockets and OAuth integrations at DonateSystem

coverage.requirement: Experience using AI tools effectively in web development
coverage.evidence: Claude and Codex listed explicitly in AI Tools skills section

coverage.requirement: Readiness to participate in architecture discussions and suggest improvements
coverage.evidence: Monorepo architecture design at KYC Market; data-fetching refactor and code audit at AMO FINTECH

tailored.match_notes: Strong overall match. The candidate covers virtually every must-have requirement with direct, production-proven evidence: React (5+ years), Next.js with SSR, TypeScript, GraphQL + Apollo Client, TailwindCSS, Jest/Vitest, WebSockets, Sentry, and performance optimization with verified metrics. The iGaming/SaaS domain is not a direct match, but the candidate's marketplace and SaaS product experience (crypto marketplace, fintech SaaS) is the closest transferable context and should resonate well. The one real gap is feature flags and trunk-based development — this is a nice-to-have, not a blocker, but worth mentioning if it comes up in the interview. DevTools proficiency is implied but not explicitly stated — be ready to speak to it. The seniority normalization (Senior → Front-End Developer) is applied throughout to align with the vacancy's middle-level framing; the candidate's depth of experience will naturally stand out positively in the interview.

level_checks.current_text: Designed and implemented a monorepo architecture using Nx to unify three products — marketplace, admin dashboard, and landing pages — maximizing UI component and business logic reuse
level_checks.explanation: 'Designed and implemented a monorepo architecture' signals org-level architectural ownership (Architected / Defined system design). For a middle-level target, this reads as a senior/staff-level initiative — designing the overarching architecture of a multi-product system is beyond the expected scope.
level_checks.suggestion: Developed and configured a monorepo setup using Nx to unify three products — marketplace, admin dashboard, and landing pages — maximizing UI component and business logic reuse

level_checks.current_text: Contributed to CI/CD pipeline configuration, production monitoring setup, and code review workflows to maintain delivery quality
Integrated GraphQL API via Apollo Client across the product, optimizing queries and caching strategies to reduce redundant network requests
Refactored the data-fetching layer using TanStack Query, adding caching, background revalidation, and consistent loading-state handling across the application
Integrated REST APIs and WebSockets for global state management using Redux, supporting reliable real-time alert distribution
level_checks.explanation: 'Integrated REST APIs and WebSockets' uses a junior-level integration verb with no indication of design decisions, performance considerations, or ownership. For a middle-level target, this bullet lacks the depth of ownership or optimization framing expected.
level_checks.suggestion: Owned real-time data flow by integrating REST APIs and WebSockets with Redux for global state management, ensuring reliable real-time alert distribution across the platform

level_checks.current_text: Delivered pixel-perfect, cross-browser layouts from Figma designs using SCSS, ensuring consistent performance across all device sizes
level_checks.explanation: 'Delivered pixel-perfect layouts from Figma designs' is a classic junior/intern-level framing — it describes execution of design specs rather than any engineering judgment, optimization, or ownership. Middle-level candidates are expected to show more than faithful implementation.
level_checks.suggestion: Built responsive, cross-browser UI components from Figma designs using SCSS, enforcing consistent rendering and performance across all device sizes and browsers

level_checks.current_text: Built an SEO-optimized marketing landing page using Next.js with semantic HTML5, custom meta tags, and responsive layouts to maximize search ranking and conversion
level_checks.explanation: 'Built an SEO-optimized marketing landing page' uses a junior-level verb ('Built') and describes a single-page deliverable with no indication of technical decisions, performance outcomes, or broader impact. The same task at AMO FINTECH (experience[1].bullets[0]) is framed more strongly.
level_checks.suggestion: Developed and optimized a marketing landing page with Next.js (SSR), implementing semantic HTML5, structured metadata, and responsive layouts that improved search rankings and supported user acquisition

level_checks.current_text: Developed data-heavy analytical dashboards with responsive tables (1000+ rows), pagination, transaction filtering, and interactive financial charts for real-time revenue tracking
level_checks.explanation: 'Developed data-heavy analytical dashboards' lists features built but shows no ownership of performance trade-offs, architecture decisions, or measurable outcomes. For a middle-level target, the framing should reflect engineering judgment (e.g., virtualization, rendering strategy) rather than a feature list.
level_checks.suggestion: Designed and delivered data-heavy analytical dashboards featuring virtualized tables (1000+ rows), pagination, transaction filtering, and interactive financial charts — optimizing rendering performance for real-time revenue tracking

stack_checks.suggestion: Add a brief qualifier in the summary or in a DonateSystem bullet, e.g., 'leveraging ES6+ features (destructuring, async/await, optional chaining) to keep the codebase clean and maintainable', to move it from a skills-list entry to demonstrated usage.

Weave TypeScript into at least one experience bullet — e.g., in the KYC Market monorepo bullet: '…enforcing strict TypeScript contracts across shared packages to prevent cross-product type regressions.' This turns a listed skill into demonstrated practice.

Add a testing bullet to the KYC Market or AMO FINTECH role, e.g., 'Wrote unit and integration tests with Jest and React Testing Library, maintaining >80% coverage on critical business-logic modules.' This demonstrates Jest in context rather than just listing it.
Strengthen the existing KYC Market CI/CD bullet to explicitly name Git: e.g., '…including Git branching strategy (trunk-based), CI/CD pipeline configuration, and code review workflows…'

recommendations.text: Reframe the DonateSystem bullets (experience[2].bullets[0], [1], [3], [4]) using middle-level ownership verbs — replace 'Built', 'Integrated', 'Delivered pixel-perfect' with 'Developed', 'Owned', 'Designed and delivered' — and add at least one measurable outcome or engineering decision per bullet to signal the expected level of judgment.

Soften the KYC Market monorepo bullet (experience[0].bullets[0]): replace 'Designed and implemented a monorepo architecture' with 'Developed and configured a monorepo setup using Nx…' to avoid the senior/staff-level 'architected the system' signal that overshoots the middle target.

Add a dedicated testing bullet to the KYC Market or AMO FINTECH role demonstrating Jest (and/or Vitest + React Testing Library) in context — e.g., coverage targets, what was tested, and the outcome. Jest is a must-have requirement and is currently only listed in top_skills, which scores as 'mentioned_only'.

Demonstrate TypeScript in at least one experience bullet. The strongest natural fit is the KYC Market monorepo bullet — add a clause about enforcing strict TypeScript contracts across shared Nx packages. TypeScript is a must-have and currently appears only in top_skills.

Explicitly name DevTools (Chrome DevTools, Performance/Network panels) in the AMO FINTECH performance bullet (experience[1].bullets[3]). The job description specifically calls out debugging tools, and the current framing ('LCP, TTI through code-splitting') implies profiling work without naming the tooling — a quick addition closes this gap cleanly.

experience_check.verdict: CV загалом добре відповідає вакансії Middle Front-End Developer: ключові технології (Next.js SSR, GraphQL + Apollo Client, TailwindCSS, CI/CD) підкріплені конкретними прикладами з досвіду, а метрики продуктивності додають переконливості. Найкритичніша проблема — мовний рівень досвіду в DonateSystem (4 з 5 буллетів звучать як junior-рівень) і один overshooting-буллет у KYC Market, що разом суттєво знижують оцінку відповідності сеньйорності; крім того, Jest і TypeScript — обидва must-have — присутні лише у списку навичок без демонстрації в досвіді.
